data.title: OpenAI CEO says ICE has 'gone too far,' Anthropic CEO calls it a 'horror'
data.description:
As the tragic events in Minnesota unfold, some in the tech community have started to speak up.

OpenAI (OPENAI) CEO Sam Altman told employees in an internal Slack message — obtained by the New York Times — that what is transpiring with the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, colloquially known as ICE, has “gone too far.”

Here is Altman's message in its entirety:

I love the US and its values of democracy and freedom and will be supportive of the country however I can; OpenAI will too. But part of loving the country is the American duty to push back against overreach. What’s happening with ICE is going too far. There is a big difference between deporting violent criminals and what’s happening now, and we need to get the distinction right.

President Trump is a very strong leader, and I hope he will rise to this moment and unite the country. I am encouraged by the last few hours of response and hope to see trust rebuilt with transparent investigations.

As a company, we aim to stick to our convictions and not get blown around by changing fashions too much. We didn’t become super woke when that was popular, we didn’t start talking about masculine corporate energy when that was popular, and we are not going to make a lot of performative statements now about safety or politics or anything else. But we are going to continue to try to figure out how to actually do the right thing as best as we can, engage with leaders and push for our values, and speak up clearly about it as needed.

Additionally, the Times reported that Altman's message received “hundreds” of supportive responses; Altman said in the comments that he had spoken to officials in the Trump Administration about the matter. 

Separately, on Monday, Anthropic (ANTHRO) CEO Dario Amodei said that what was going on in Minnesota was a “horror,” and a recent missive from him “is particularly relevant,” given it focuses “on the importance of preserving democratic values and rights at home.” 

Earlier this week, the CEOs of a number of Minnesota-based companies called for calm after Alex Pretti, a nurse, was wrestled to the ground and was shot by several ICE officers. Pretti's death came weeks after agents killed Renee Good in Minneapolis.

Seeking Alpha has sent requests for comments to a number of major technology firms on the matter.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Snowflake launches energy solutions
data.description:
Snowflake (SNOW) on Tuesday announced new energy solutions that enable companies to build data foundation for AI by connecting data across IT, OT, and IoT systems.

The aim is to enable power, utilities, and oil and gas companies to modernize infrastructure, improve efficiency, and accelerate progress toward a more reliable and lower-carbon future.

The company said the launch builds on Snowflake's new partnership with SAP, enabling energy companies to combine SAP finance and supply chain data with operational and field data on Snowflake.

"Data is the control plane for the future of energy," said Fred Cohagan, global head of energy at Snowflake.

"Whether it's keeping the grid secure, protecting critical assets, or balancing supply and demand in volatile markets, energy companies need a trusted data foundation that can activate AI everywhere," added Cohagan.

data.title: AT&T, Verizon Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
When AT&T (T) posts quarterly results on Wednesday, the U.S. wireless carrier’s wireless subscribers and margin figure are expected to capture investors’ focus.

Wall Street expects the Texas-based company to post EPS of $0.47, on revenue of $32.88 billion, implying a rise of nearly 2% during the quarter.

AT&T, in October, added more wireless subscribers than expected, as it managed to attract more customers due to its heavy promotional activities around the new iPhone launch. However, the company experienced in-line profit and revenue misses, leading to the stock falling over 8% since its third-quarter results.

“With AT&T's revenue expected to grow at a rate within 1-2%, a drop in gross margin could have significant implications for earnings per share in 2025, and that is why the ARPU gross margin dynamic will be of crucial importance within the upcoming report,” noted Seeking Alpha analyst Vladimir Dimitrov.

Over the last three months, AT&T’s EPS estimates have seen four upward revisions, compared to three downward revisions. Revenue estimates have been revised upwards four times versus five downward moves.

Verizon’s third quarter saw a wide topline miss, a small bottomline beat, and subscriber losses in its consumer business. Still, new CEO Dan Schulman’s strategy to move towards a “customer-centric growth” with a goal to "have the lowest churn rate in the industry" boosted investor confidence.

Wall Street expects Verizon to post EPS of $1.06 on revenue of $36.2 billion. 

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis by Juxtaposed Ideas argued that the “aggressive headcount reduction and pivot to franchised retail operations are expected to drive improved productivity and profitability starting in FY2026.”

“I am of the opinion that the market may be in a wait-and-see mode, with investors likely to closely monitor their upcoming FY2026 guidance in the FQ4'25 earnings call sometime in January 2026,” it added.

Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are bullish and rated both the stocks a Buy. In contrast, Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings are cautious and consider them a Hold.

Over the last three months, Verizon’s EPS estimates have seen no upward revisions, compared to 12 downward revisions, while revenue estimates have seen one upward revision versus 10 downward moves.

Over the last two years, Verizon has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time. Meanwhile, AT&T has beaten both revenue and EPS estimates 63% of the time.

data.title: UK politicians call for competition review of Netflix bid for Warner Bros. - FT
data.description:
Several UK politicians and former policymakers are calling for the UK's competition regulator to start a “full review” of Netflix's (NFLX) $83 billion deal for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD). 

The officials fear that the deal "will cement an already dominant player" in the TV streaming market, according to a Financial Times report on Tuesday, which cited a letter sent to Sarah Cardell, chief executive of the UK's Competition and Markets Authority. 

The letter was signed by Lord Chris Smith, Sir Oliver Dowden, and Dame Karen Bradley, who have all acted as secretary of state for culture, media and sport, as well as Lord Tony Hall, a former director-general of the BBC. 

The officials warned that a combination could cause a “chilling impact on the streaming market,'' also adding: “At a time when the British consumer can ill-afford more price increases, Netflix would possess an unprecedented ability to raise prices to access television and films.”

Netflix responded to the FT.

 “We are in contact with regulatory bodies in all relevant jurisdictions, including the CMA," Netflix said. "We’re highly confident in the regulatory process.This deal is pro-consumer, pro-innovation, pro-worker, pro-creator, and pro-growth.”

data.title: Deutsche upgrades CoreWeave to Buy on 2026 setup, Nvidia deal
data.description:
Deutsche Bank upgraded CoreWeave (CRWV) to Buy from Hold due to a combination of its setup entering 2026 and Monday's announcement that Nvidia (NVDA) has invested $2B in the hyperscaler and expanded their collaboration to help CoreWeave accelerate the buildout of more than 5 gigawatts of AI factories by 2030.

CoreWeave shares popped up 7% during Tuesday morning market action.

"We firmly believe this was not about CoreWeave's need for capital to fund its growth but more strategic in nature," said Deutsche analysts, led by Brad Zelnick, in a Tuesday investor note. "In addition to being further validation of its technical leadership, it provides CoreWeave three key things: (1) an accelerated path to its newly stated goal of adding 5GW+ of Al Infrastructure by 2030 (beyond current 2.9GW backlog), leveraging NVIDIA's financial strength to secure access to additional land and powered shells; (2) deepened technical integration that should enable CoreWeave to remain among the very first to market with new NVIDIA compute architectures; (3) potential for the company's SUNK and Mission Control software to be included in NVIDIA's reference architectures, opening up a sizeble and high margin standalone hybrid and multi-cloud SW opportunity over time."

Deutsche also increased CoreWeave's price target to $140 from $100.

"We believe the company is also entering the sweet spot over the next couple of quarters to start booking new business on its >1GW of secured power still available to contract out to customers in a supply-constrained market, which, along with their track record of being early to market with new platforms (Rubin upcoming) puts them in a very advantageous position," Zelnick said.

The investment bank will be closely monitoring CoreWeave's fourth quarter 2025 results and 2026 guidance. CoreWeave is slated to release its latest earnings post-market on March 3. It will be the company's fourth earnings call since going public. A consensus estimate expects non-GAAP earnings per share of ($0.49), GAAP EPS of ($0.67) and revenue of $1.54B.

data.title: Amazon transitioning Go and Fresh stores to Whole Foods
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is closing its remaining Amazon Go and Amazon Fresh stores, repurposing those locations as Whole Foods markets and ramping up investment in online grocery delivery to meet growing consumer demand for fresh foods.

“Everyday household essentials represent one out of every three units sold on Amazon.com, as customers increasingly look to our broad selection, low prices, and fast delivery to fill their fridges and pantries,” the company said in a statement, noting the “remarkable” growth in its online grocery delivery, demonstrating growth of 40x over the past year.

As Amazon transitions its remaining Amazon Go and Amazon Fresh stores, it plans to expand Whole Foods with 100 new locations over the next few years and also add five more Whole Foods Market Daily Shop locations by year-end. The Daily Shop format offers a small, convenience-style store focused on grab-and-go meals, coffee, and everyday essentials.

Finally, to match the physical scale of competitor Walmart (WMT), Amazon (AMZN) is exploring a supercenter retail concept to shop the company’s broad selection of merchandise, including fresh groceries and household essentials.

data.title: Dynex Capital announces increase in shares available for sale by 60M
data.description:
Dynex Capital (DX) on Tuesday announced an amendment to its distribution agreement, which increases the number of shares available for sale by 60M to 221.29M shares of common stock.

The company said 67.35M of the shares remain available for issuance.

data.title: Kazakhstan's biggest oilfield seen recovering less than half its output by February 7 - Reuters
data.description:
The Tengiz oilfield, one of the world's biggest oilfields and Kazakhstan's biggest, likely will restore less than half of its normal production by February 7, Reuters reported Tuesday, as it slowly recovers from a fire and power outage.

The Chevron-led (CVX) operator of the field temporarily halted production at the Tengiz and Korolev oilfields on January 18 after a fire at a generator caused a power supply outage.

Chevron (CVX) said oil production had restarted, without naming the field, and Kazakhstan's energy ministry said separately that Tengiz was preparing to resume oil output soon while Korolev was already operating.

The fields are expected to produce 57K metric tons/day by February 7, or ~46% of normal levels, one source told Reuters, while another said Tengiz production may reach 670K bbl/day by mid-February, which is near autumn output levels but well below the 900K bbl/day the field reached early last year.

Chevron (CVX) is operator and the largest shareholder in the Kazakhstan consortium with a 50% stake, followed by Exxon Mobil (XOM) with 25%, Kazakhstan's KazMunayGaz with 20%, and Russia's Lukoil at 5%.

data.title: Top performing industrials stocks in the past month
data.description:
As the first month of 2026 comes to an end this week and earnings season accelerates, below is a list of the top 10 industrials stocks ranked by their one-month price performance percentage.

The list is topped by FTAI Aviation (FTAI), with a one-month performance of 65.87%. Bloom Energy (BE) and Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) follow closely, demonstrating strong price momentum with gains of 54.82% and 43.27%, respectively.

Notably, L3Harris Technologies (LHX) holds the highest Quant Rating on the list at Strong Buy (4.60), despite ranking 6th in performance. Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) and CACI International (CACI) also secured Buy ratings from the Quant system, with ratings of 4.17 and 3.87, respectively.

Here is the list:

  1. FTAI Aviation (FTAI), 1 month performance percentage: 65.87%

  2. Bloom Energy (BE), 1 month performance percentage: 54.82%

  3. Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS), 1 month performance percentage: 43.27%

  4. Karman Holdings (KRMN), 1 month performance percentage: 40.18%

  5. Lockheed Martin (LMT), 1 month performance percentage: 20.42%

  6. L3Harris Technologies (LHX), 1 month performance percentage: 19.35%

  7. AeroVironment (AVAV), 1 month performance percentage: 18.69%

  8. CACI International (CACI), 1 month performance percentage: 18.60%

  9. Leonardo DRS (DRS), 1 month performance percentage: 18.52%

  10. Huntington Ingalls (HII), 1 month performance percentage: 17.78%

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN)
